chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1347 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For katcha peshi. 3. For hearing of MA-2659/2017 15.09.2017 Mr. Gulab Khan Qaimkhani, Advocate for petitioners alongwith petitioner No.2. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Mahar, State Counsel a/w Sohail Khaskheli SHO Nauabad District Sanghar and Muhammad Mithal SHO P.S Sukkur himself and o/b of SSP Sukkur. Respondents No.7 and 9 are present in person. = With reference to the order of previous date, Mr. Akhlaque Ahmed Baloch, Advocate has appeared and files power on behalf of respondents No.5 and 6 which is taken on record. Respondents No.5, 6, 7 and 9 are present in Court and state that neither they harassed the petitioners in the past nor they intend to harass them in future. Learned State Counsel files parawise comments on behalf of respondents No.1, 2, 3, 4, 7 and 9, which are taken on record, copies whereof have been supplied to learned Counsel for the petitioners. In the comments, the official respondents have denied the allegations leveled against them by the petitioners, however, the learned State Counsel undertakes that the official respondents shall act strictly in accordance with law. Learned Counsel for the petitioners, upon undertakings given by the State Counsel as well as private respondents, is satisfied and seeks disposal of this petition with necessary direction to the respondents not to cause harassment. In view of the above, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and in the event the petitioners approach them for legal protection the same shall be provided but strictly in accordance with law.

Learned Counsel for the petitioners, upon undertakings given by the State Counsel as well as private respondents, is satisfied and seeks disposal of this petition with necessary direction to the respondents not to cause harassment. In view of the above, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and in the event the petitioners approach them for legal protection the same shall be provided but strictly in accordance with law. However, if the private respondent No.5, who claims to be husband of petitioner No.1, has any grievance against the petitioners, the same shall be redressed through due process of law but not by causing harassment to the petitioners. JUDGE Shahid
